# SlintORM

> Minimal, fully-typed TypeScript ORM for Node.js, serverless functions, and V8 isolate edge runtimes (Vercel Edge, Cloudflare Workers, Deno Deploy).

- GitHub: https://github.com/emeraldlinks/slintorm
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/slintorm
- Author: Joseph Christopher
- Organization: emeraldlinks
- License: MIT
- Version: 1.1.4

---

## Overview

SlintORM is a TypeScript ORM that works across all JavaScript runtimes without additional configuration. It supports SQLite, PostgreSQL, MySQL, and MongoDB, provides a fluent query builder, automatic migrations, relation preloading, lifecycle hooks, validation, and structured error handling.

Key design goals:
- Zero Node.js-specific imports at the module top level — safe to import in V8 isolate runtimes
- Schema is pre-generated at build time and passed as a plain object — no filesystem access at runtime
- All driver imports are dynamic (`import()`) so bundlers tree-shake unused drivers
- `migrate()` is a Node-only build-step function and is never called in edge runtimes

---

## Installation

```bash
npm install slintorm

# Install only the driver you need:
npm install pg          # PostgreSQL
npm install mysql2      # MySQL
npm install mongodb     # MongoDB
npm install better-sqlite3   # SQLite (preferred, sync)
# or
npm install sqlite3 sqlite   # SQLite (async fallback)
```

All database drivers are optional peer dependencies.

---

## Quick Start

### Node.js (with migrate)

```typescript
import ORMManager from 'slintorm';

const orm = new ORMManager({
  driver: 'postgres',
  databaseUrl: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  dir: 'src', // directory containing your TypeScript models
});

await orm.migrate(); // reads TypeScript files, generates schema, runs DDL

const Users = await orm.defineModel<User>('user', 'User');
const Posts = await orm.defineModel<Post>('post', 'Post');
```

### Serverless / Edge (with pre-built schema)

```typescript
// Run at build time: npx slintorm generate
// Commit the generated file: src/schema/generated.json

import ORMManager from 'slintorm';
import schema from './src/schema/generated.json' assert { type: 'json' };

const orm = new ORMManager({
  driver: 'postgres',
  databaseUrl: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  schema, // pre-built — no filesystem access needed
});

// Do NOT call orm.migrate() in edge functions
const Users = await orm.defineModel<User>('user', 'User');
```

### Functional API

```typescript
import { createORM } from 'slintorm';

const { defineModel } = await createORM({
  driver: 'postgres',
  databaseUrl: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  schema,
});

const Users = defineModel<User>('user', 'User');
```

---

## Configuration

```typescript
new ORMManager({
  driver: 'postgres' | 'mysql' | 'sqlite' | 'mongodb', // required
  databaseUrl: string,           // connection string or file path
  databaseName: string,          // MongoDB only
  dir: string,                   // source directory for schema generation (default: 'src')
  logs: boolean,                 // log all SQL to console (default: false)
  schema: Record<string, SchemaModel>, // pre-built schema for edge runtimes
})
```

---

## Defining Models

```typescript
// TypeScript class/type
type User = {
  id?: number;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  role?: string;
  active?: boolean;
  meta?: object;
  createdAt?: string;
  updatedAt?: string;
  deletedAt?: string; // enables soft delete when present
};

const Users = await orm.defineModel<User>('user', 'User');
// Registers as orm.DB.User too:
// orm.DB.User === Users
```

---

## CRUD Operations

### insert

```typescript
const user = await Users.insert({
  name: 'Alice',
  email: 'alice@example.com',
  role: 'admin',
});
// Returns EntityWithUpdate<User> | null
// createdAt and updatedAt are set automatically
```

### get

```typescript
const user = await Users.get({ id: 1 });
const user = await Users.get({ email: 'alice@example.com' });
// Returns EntityWithUpdate<User> | null
// Throws if filter is empty {}
```

### getAll

```typescript
const users = await Users.getAll();
// Returns User[]
```

### update

```typescript
const updated = await Users.update({ id: 1 }, { name: 'Alice Smith' });
// Returns EntityWithUpdate<User> | null
// updatedAt is set automatically
// Throws if filter or data is empty
```

### delete

```typescript
await Users.delete({ id: 1 });
// Throws if filter is empty
```

### Entity methods (on records returned by get/insert)

```typescript
const user = await Users.get({ id: 1 });

// Update via entity — uses primary key, not original filter
await user.update({ name: 'Bob' });

// Refresh — returns latest from DB using primary key
const fresh = await user.refresh();
// NOTE: refresh() uses the primary key (not the original filter)
// so it works correctly after updating non-PK fields

// Delete via entity
await user.delete();

// Serialize
const plain = user.toJSON();
```

---

## Bulk Operations

```typescript
// Insert multiple rows in a single transaction
const count = await Users.insertMany([
  { name: 'Alice', email: 'alice@example.com' },
  { name: 'Bob',   email: 'bob@example.com'   },
]);
// Returns number of rows inserted

// Update multiple rows matching filter
const changes = await Users.updateMany({ role: 'guest' }, { role: 'user' });
// Returns number of affected rows

// Delete multiple rows matching filter
const deleted = await Users.deleteMany({ active: false });
// Returns number of deleted rows

// Delete all rows
await Users.truncate();
```

---

## Aggregates

```typescript
const total  = await Users.count();
const active = await Users.count({ role: 'admin' });

const maxAge = await Users.max('age');
const minAge = await Users.min('age');
const avgAge = await Users.avg('age');
const sumAge = await Users.sum('age');

// With filter
const avgAdminAge = await Users.avg('age', { role: 'admin' });
```

---

## Upsert and findOrCreate

```typescript
// Insert or update
const result = await Users.upsert(
  { email: 'alice@example.com' },     // match filter
  { email: 'alice@example.com', name: 'Alice' } // full data
);
// Returns 'inserted' | 'updated'

// Find or create
const { record, created } = await Users.findOrCreate(
  { email: 'alice@example.com' },     // match filter
  { email: 'alice@example.com', name: 'Alice' } // defaults if creating
);
// created: boolean
```

---

## exists

```typescript
const exists = await Users.exists({ email: 'alice@example.com' });
// Returns boolean
```

---

## Query Builder

Access via `model.query()`. All methods are chainable. Call `.get()` or `.first()` to execute.

### Basic filters

```typescript
// Equality
Users.query().where('role', '=', 'admin').get()

// Comparison operators: = | != | < | <= | > | >= | LIKE | ILIKE | IN | NOT IN
Users.query().where('age', '>', 18).get()
Users.query().where('name', 'LIKE', 'Ali%').get()

// OR condition
Users.query()
  .where('role', '=', 'admin')
  .orWhere('role', '=', 'moderator')
  .get()

// Raw WHERE with params (e.g. for subqueries)
// IMPORTANT: always pass params array to avoid them being silently dropped
Users.query().whereRaw('"age" > ?', [18]).get()

// IN / NOT IN
Users.query().whereIn('role', ['admin', 'moderator']).get()
Users.query().whereNotIn('status', ['banned', 'deleted']).get()

// NULL checks
Users.query().whereNull('deletedAt').get()
Users.query().whereNotNull('verifiedAt').get()

// BETWEEN
Users.query().whereBetween('age', 18, 65).get()

// Case-insensitive LIKE (emulated on SQLite, native ILIKE on Postgres)
Users.query().ILike('name', 'alice').get()
// Matches 'alice', 'Alice', 'ALICE' etc.
```

### Column selection and projection

```typescript
Users.query().select('id', 'name', 'email').get()

Users.query().distinct('role').get()
Users.query().distinct().select('role', 'status').get()

// Exclude specific columns
Users.query().exclude('password', 'secret').get()

// Exclude nested relation fields (used with preload)
Users.query().preload('posts').exclude('posts.internalNote').get()
```

### Sorting, pagination

```typescript
Users.query().orderBy('createdAt', 'desc').get()
Users.query().orderBy('name', 'asc').limit(10).get()
Users.query().limit(10).offset(20).get()

// Paginate — page is 1-indexed
Users.query().paginate(2, 10).get() // page 2, 10 per page

// getPaginated — returns { data, total, page, lastPage }
const result = await Users.query()
  .where('active', '=', true)
  .getPaginated(1, 20);
// result.data: User[]
// result.total: number
// result.page: number
// result.lastPage: number
```

### first()

```typescript
const user = await Users.query().where('role', '=', 'admin').first()
// Returns T | null

// With inline condition object
const user = await Users.query().first({ email: 'alice@example.com' })

// With raw SQL condition
const user = await Users.query().first('"createdAt" > \'2024-01-01\'')
```

### Aggregates in query builder

These add aggregate columns to the SELECT — they do NOT return a number directly. Chain `.get()` to get rows with the computed column. For a plain number use `model.count()` / `model.sum()` etc.

```typescript
Users.query().countAggregate('id').get()
// [{ 'COUNT(id)': 5 }]

Sales.query()
  .select('region')
  .sum('amount')
  .groupBy('region')
  .get()
// [{ region: 'North', 'SUM(amount)': 1200 }, ...]

Sales.query()
  .select('region')
  .sum('amount')
  .groupBy('region')
  .having('SUM(amount) > 1000')
  .get()

// Other aggregates
Users.query().avg('age').get()
Users.query().min('score').get()
Users.query().max('score').get()
```

### Window functions

```typescript
Users.query()
  .window('ROW_NUMBER()', 'PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC')
  .get()
// Adds a window column to each row result
```

### JOINs

```typescript
// 4-argument signature: (table, leftCol, operator, rightCol)
Posts.query()
  .join('users', 'posts.userId', '=', 'users.id')
  .where('users.name', '=', 'Alice')  // dotted table.col refs work correctly
  .get()

Posts.query()
  .leftJoin('users', 'posts.userId', '=', 'users.id')
  .get()

Posts.query()
  .rightJoin('categories', 'posts.categoryId', '=', 'categories.id')
  .get()

Posts.query()
  .fullOuterJoin('comments', 'posts.id', '=', 'comments.postId')
  .get()
```

### Subqueries

```typescript
// Subquery in whereRaw — always pass the subquery params
const activeSub = Users.query().select('id').where('active', '=', true);
const { sql, params } = activeSub.buildSql();
Posts.query().whereRaw(`"userId" IN (${sql})`, params).get()

// Subquery as a selected column
Posts.query()
  .selectSubquery(
    Comments.query().countAggregate('id'),
    'commentCount'
  )
  .get()

// EXISTS / NOT EXISTS
Posts.query()
  .exists(Comments.query().where('postId', '=', 1))
  .get()

Posts.query()
  .notExists(Reports.query().where('postId', '=', 1))
  .get()

// UNION
const sql = AdvancedQueryBuilder.union([
  Users.query().where('role', '=', 'admin'),
  Users.query().where('role', '=', 'moderator'),
])
// Returns combined SQL string; execute manually with model.raw()
```

### Scopes

```typescript
// Define reusable query fragments
const activeScope   = (qb) => qb.where('active', '=', true);
const adminScope    = (qb) => qb.where('role', '=', 'admin');

Users.query()
  .scope(activeScope)
  .scope(adminScope)
  .get()
```

### Relation traversal in WHERE

```typescript
// whereRelated(relationFieldName, column, value)
// Resolves to an IN subquery — no JOIN required
Posts.query().whereRelated('user', 'name', 'Alice').get()
// Finds all posts whose user.name = 'Alice'

// relatedTo(targetModelName, column, value)
// Auto-discovers the relation path via BFS
Posts.query().relatedTo('User', 'role', 'admin').get()
```

### Joining through relations

```typescript
// throughRelation adds the necessary JOINs based on schema relations
Posts.query()
  .throughRelation('user')
  .select('posts.id', 'posts.title', 'users.name')
  .get()
```

---

## Soft Delete

Add a `deletedAt` field to your model schema. The query builder automatically excludes soft-deleted rows unless you opt in.

```typescript
type Post = {
  id?: number;
  title: string;
  deletedAt?: string | null; // presence enables soft delete
};

// Default: excludes deleted rows
Posts.query().get()

// Include deleted rows
Posts.query().withTrashed().get()

// Only deleted rows
Posts.query().onlyTrashed().get()

// Soft-delete (set deletedAt)
await Posts.update({ id: 1 }, { deletedAt: new Date().toISOString() });

// Restore (clear deletedAt)
await Posts.restore({ id: 1 });
```

---

## Relation Preloading

Define relations in your schema. Then use `.preload()` on the query builder.

### Relation kinds

- `onetomany` — parent has many children (foreignKey on child)
- `manytoone` — child belongs to parent (foreignKey on child)
- `onetoone` — one record linked to one other
- `manytomany` — linked through a pivot table

### Examples

```typescript
// onetomany
const users = await Users.query().preload('posts').get();
// users[0].posts = Post[]

// manytoone
const posts = await Posts.query().preload('user').get();
// posts[0].user = User | null

// onetoone
const users = await Users.query().preload('profile').get();
// users[0].profile = Profile | null

// manytomany
const users = await Users.query().preload('tags').get();
// users[0].tags = Tag[]

// Nested preload
const users = await Users.query().preload('posts.comments').get();
// users[0].posts[0].comments = Comment[]

// Combine preloads
const users = await Users.query()
  .preload('posts')
  .preload('profile')
  .get();

// Exclude nested fields
const users = await Users.query()
  .preload('posts')
  .exclude('posts.internalNote', 'posts.draft')
  .get();
```

---

## Lifecycle Hooks

```typescript
const Users = await orm.defineModel<User>('user', 'User', {
  onCreateBefore: async (item) => {
    // Transform item before insert; return modified item or void
    return { ...item, name: item.name.trim() };
  },
  onCreateAfter: async (item) => {
    // item is the fully inserted record
    await sendWelcomeEmail(item.email);
  },
  onUpdateBefore: async (oldData, newData) => {
    // Return modified newData or void
    return { ...newData, updatedBy: currentUserId };
  },
  onUpdateAfter: async (oldData, newData) => {
    await auditLog('update', oldData, newData);
  },
  onDeleteBefore: async (record) => {
    await archiveRecord(record);
  },
  onDeleteAfter: async (record) => {
    await clearCache(record.id);
  },
});
```

---

## Validation

```typescript
const rules = {
  email: {
    required: true,
    email: true,           // must match email format
  },
  name: {
    required: true,
    minLength: 2,
    maxLength: 100,
  },
  age: {
    min: 0,
    max: 120,
  },
  username: {
    match: /^[a-z0-9_]+$/,
    custom: (value, row) => {
      if (value === 'admin') return 'Username "admin" is reserved';
    },
  },
};

// Throws ValidationError
Users.validate(data, rules);

// Returns errors object or null (does not throw)
const errors = Users.check(data, rules);
if (errors) {
  console.log(errors); // { email: 'email must be a valid email', ... }
}
```

```typescript
import { ValidationError } from 'slintorm';

try {
  Users.validate(data, rules);
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof ValidationError) {
    console.log(err.errors); // Record<string, string>
  }
}
```

---

## Error Handling

All constraint violations are wrapped in `ORMError` with structured metadata.

```typescript
import { ORMError } from 'slintorm';

try {
  await Users.insert({ email: 'existing@example.com', name: 'Alice' });
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof ORMError) {
    err.ormCode  // 'UNIQUE_VIOLATION' | 'NOT_NULL_VIOLATION' | 'FOREIGN_KEY_VIOLATION' | 'CHECK_VIOLATION'
    err.message  // Human-readable explanation + tip
    err.table    // Table name (if parseable)
    err.column   // Column name (if parseable)
    err.value    // Conflicting value (if parseable)
    err.original // Original driver error
  }
}
```

Error codes:
- `UNIQUE_VIOLATION` — duplicate value in a unique/primary key column
- `NOT_NULL_VIOLATION` — null provided for a NOT NULL column
- `FOREIGN_KEY_VIOLATION` — referenced record does not exist
- `CHECK_VIOLATION` — value rejected by a CHECK constraint

Works across SQLite, Postgres, and MySQL.

---

## Transactions

```typescript
await orm.transaction(async (trx) => {
  await trx.exec('INSERT INTO "orders" ("userId","total") VALUES (?,?)', [1, 99.99]);
  await trx.exec('UPDATE "users" SET "balance" = "balance" - ? WHERE "id" = ?', [99.99, 1]);
  // Automatically committed on success, rolled back on throw
});

// batch — executes multiple statements in one transaction
await orm.batch([
  { sql: 'INSERT INTO "log" ("message") VALUES (?)', params: ['started'] },
  { sql: 'UPDATE "jobs" SET "status" = ? WHERE "id" = ?', params: ['done', 42] },
]);
```

---

## Migrations

Migrations are a Node.js build-step — never run in edge/serverless functions.

```typescript
// In a Node.js script (e.g. scripts/migrate.ts)
const orm = new ORMManager({ driver: 'postgres', databaseUrl, dir: 'src' });
await orm.migrate();
// Reads TypeScript model files in src/
// Generates src/schema/generated.json
// Creates/alters tables as needed
process.exit(0);
```

```json
// package.json
{
  "scripts": {
    "migrate": "tsx scripts/migrate.ts",
    "build": "npm run migrate && next build"
  }
}
```

For edge runtimes, generate the schema separately:

```bash
npx slintorm generate
```

Then import and pass it:

```typescript
import schema from './src/schema/generated.json' assert { type: 'json' };
const orm = new ORMManager({ driver: 'postgres', databaseUrl, schema });
```

---

## Serverless & Edge Runtime Support

| Feature | Node.js | Vercel Serverless | Vercel Edge | Cloudflare Workers | Deno |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Postgres driver | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅* | ✅ |
| MySQL driver | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅* | ✅ |
| MongoDB driver | ✅ | ✅ | ⚠️ | ⚠️ | ✅ |
| SQLite driver | ✅ | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ✅ |
| orm.migrate() | ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ |
| Pre-built schema | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |

\* Requires a TCP-compatible connection pooler (e.g. Cloudflare Hyperdrive or Neon serverless driver).

### Edge example (Next.js App Router)

```typescript
// app/api/users/route.ts
export const runtime = 'edge';

import ORMManager from 'slintorm';
import schema from '@/src/schema/generated.json' assert { type: 'json' };

const orm = new ORMManager({
  driver: 'postgres',
  databaseUrl: process.env.DATABASE_URL!,
  schema,
});

const Users = await orm.defineModel('user', 'User');

export async function GET() {
  const users = await Users.query()
    .where('active', '=', true)
    .orderBy('createdAt', 'desc')
    .limit(20)
    .get();
  return Response.json(users);
}
```

---

## TypeScript Types

```typescript
import ORMManager, {
  createORM,
  type ORMManagerConfig,
  type ModelHooks,
  type AnyModelMap,
  type DBStore,
  type ReadonlyDBStore,
} from 'slintorm';

import { type ModelAPI }      from 'slintorm/model';
import { ORMError }           from 'slintorm/db-error';
import { ValidationError }    from 'slintorm/extensions';

// EntityWithUpdate<T> — return type of get() and insert()
// Adds .update(), .delete(), .refresh(), .toJSON() to T
import type { EntityWithUpdate } from 'slintorm/types';
```

---

## Runtime Errors

| Error | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ORMError: Duplicate value` | UNIQUE constraint | Check unique fields before insert or use `upsert()` |
| `ORMError: Missing required value` | NOT NULL constraint | Ensure all required fields are provided |
| `ORMError: Invalid reference` | FOREIGN KEY constraint | Insert the referenced record first |
| `ValidationError` | `validate()` failed | Check `err.errors` for field-level messages |
| `Error: Filter must contain at least one field` | `get({})` / `delete({})` | Always provide at least one filter field |
| `Error: No SQLite driver found` | SQLite packages not installed | `npm install better-sqlite3` or `npm install sqlite3 sqlite` |
| `Error: SlintORM: cannot load schema from disk` | Edge runtime, no schema passed | Pass `schema` option with pre-built JSON |

---

## CLI

```bash
# Generate schema JSON from TypeScript model files (run at build time)
npx slintorm generate

# Or via package.json script
slintorm generate
```

---

## package.json (v1.1.4)

```json
{
  "name": "slintorm",
  "version": "1.1.4",
  "type": "module",
  "main": "./dist/src/index.js",
  "types": "./dist/src/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "types":   "./dist/src/index.d.ts",
      "browser": "./dist/src/browser.js",
      "import":  "./dist/src/index.js",
      "require": "./dist/src/index.cjs",
      "default": "./dist/src/index.js"
    },
    "./package.json": "./package.json"
  },
  "bin": { "slintorm": "./dist/src/bin/cli.js" },
  "peerDependencies": {
    "pg":             "^8.0.0",
    "mysql2":         "^3.0.0",
    "mongodb":        "^6.0.0",
    "sqlite3":        "^5.0.0",
    "sqlite":         "^5.0.0",
    "better-sqlite3": "^9.0.0"
  },
  "peerDependenciesMeta": {
    "pg":             { "optional": true },
    "mysql2":         { "optional": true },
    "mongodb":        { "optional": true },
    "sqlite3":        { "optional": true },
    "sqlite":         { "optional": true },
    "better-sqlite3": { "optional": true }
  }
}
```
